subsection.title: nth roots of a complex number
subsection.p: Let w=Re^{i\Theta}, \qquad R>0. The solutions of z^n=w are \boxed{ z_k = R^{1/n}e^{i(\Theta+2\pi k)/n}, \qquad k=0,1,\ldots,n-1. }
They lie on the circle of radius R^{1/n}.

subsection.title: Roots of unity
subsection.p: The solutions of z^n=1 are \boxed{ z_k=e^{2\pi i k/n}, \qquad k=0,1,\ldots,n-1. }
These are the nth roots of unity.
They form a regular n-gon on the unit circle.

subsection.title: Primitive root
Square roots
subsection.p: To solve z^2=Re^{i\Theta}, use \boxed{ z_0=\sqrt R e^{i\Theta/2}, \qquad z_1=\sqrt R e^{i(\Theta+2\pi)/2}. }

subsection.title: Examples
subsection.p: Solutions of z^3=8: write 8=8e^{i0}. Then z_k=2e^{2\pi i k/3}, \qquad k=0,1,2. So \boxed{ z=2,\quad -1+\sqrt3\,i,\quad -1-\sqrt3\,i. }
Solutions of z^3=-8: write -8=8e^{i\pi}. Then z_k=2e^{i(\pi+2\pi k)/3}, \qquad k=0,1,2. So \boxed{ z=1+\sqrt3\,i,\quad -2,\quad 1-\sqrt3\,i. }

subsection.title: Geometric meaning
subsection.p: The extra 2\pi k is why there are n roots.
